response.justification: Bogdan is an experienced full-stack and web-focused engineer with a broad polyglot background, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The position requires native-level Japanese proficiency, which is an explicit hard requirement — the candidate lists English, Romanian, and basic German only. Beyond that disqualifying gap, the role demands deep embedded C programming for LAN-switch firmware, Linux kernel internals, and hands-on knowledge of Layer-2/3 security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal), none of which appear in the resume. His primary experience is TypeScript/Node.js web services, GIS platforms, and developer tooling — a fundamentally different domain.
strengths: 15+ years of continuous software development experience across diverse domains and languages
Has some embedded/low-latency systems exposure (NI LabWindows C/C++, robotic arms at Altom, high-performance async I/O at Sociomantic)
Demonstrated adaptability and breadth — picks up new technologies quickly and has contributed across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded contexts
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — this is a stated hard requirement for the role
No evidence of embedded networking, LAN-switch firmware, or relevant security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, Linux kernel security)
Primary technology stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB) is entirely web/cloud-oriented and does not translate to embedded firmware development in C
